briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby North Road BIA Bylaw Would Fund Area Improvements Over Five Years
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would formally establish the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pool resources to improve the area — and approve over $1.75 million in funding to the Burnaby North Road Business Association from April 2026 to March 2031. If passed, property owners in the BIA with light industrial or commercial properties would pay a special local tax to cover these costs.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property falls within the Burnaby North Road BIA boundary — you may owe a new local tax.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw — proposed start date takes effect
Burnaby North Road BIA — annual budget submission deadline to City
Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw — five-year term ends

briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Amenity Pier Covenant for 8351 Fraser Reach Court, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a covenant — a legal agreement registered on a property title that sets out specific conditions — related to an amenity pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court in the Riverbend Business Park area near the Fraser River. The sketches presented show the subject site's location within the Big Bend Community Plan area and identify a previously proposed pier location along the riverfront.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near Fraser Reach Court, monitor this council decision — it may affect nearby land use.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Developer Forfeits $1.36M Instead of Building Fraser River Pier
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive information about a developer's decision to give up $1,363,500 in security — money held as a guarantee — rather than build a planned public pier on the Fraser River at Riverbend Business Park. The developer, Oxford Properties, could not get the required provincial land approval to build on the water lot, so under a legal agreement registered on the property, they are allowed to forfeit the funds instead. Council will consider how to use that money for a future public amenity.
briefs.action_required: Watch for how Council decides to spend the $1.36M — it may fund public amenities near your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Amenity Pier Covenant report received for information

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Progress Update on Burnaby's Economic Growth Task Force
briefs.summary: Council will consider an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience, a group of business and community leaders working to build a stronger local economy. The Task Force has held five workshops so far and is now gathering feedback from businesses through surveys and interviews, with final recommendations expected to be presented to Council on June 23, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026 — they may affect local business support programs.

key_dates.label: Mayor's Task Force final recommendations presented to Burnaby Council

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider Rezoning Prerequisites for 5035 Beresford Street Development
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a list of conditions that a developer must meet before a rezoning bylaw for 5035 Beresford Street can be officially approved. These prerequisites — meaning required steps the developer must complete first — include submitting engineering plans, signing a servicing agreement, dedicating portions of land along Beresford Street and Dunblane Avenue, and registering several legal covenants (binding promises registered on the property title) covering things like noise, stormwater, public art, affordable rental units, and connection to the City's district energy system.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this rezoning could affect nearby property owners, tenants, and contractors.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: 20-Storey Rental Apartment at 5035 Beresford Street, Burnaby — Council to Consider
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a rezoning application (REZ #25-11) that would allow a 20-storey, 215-unit market rental apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street. The proposed development includes a mix of studio, one-, two-, and three-bedroom units, 100% EV-ready parking, and on-site amenity spaces — but no final decision has been made yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on REZ #25-11 — it could affect nearby property values and rental supply.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider Rezoning Application REZ 25-11 Building Elevations
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on rezoning application REZ 25-11, which includes architectural elevation drawings (front, west, and east views) for a proposed development in Burnaby. These drawings are part of the supporting documents for the rezoning request and show what the building would look like if the application is approved.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meeting updates for the REZ 25-11 vote if this development affects your business.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rezoning REZ 25-11 — Shadow Study and Site Plan Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider rezoning application REZ 25-11, which includes a shadow study, site plan, and subdivision plan for a development project. The attached documents (pages 163–165) form part of the technical review package that Council will use to evaluate how the proposed building affects sunlight on nearby properties and how the land would be divided.
briefs.action_required: Review the shadow study and site plan for REZ 25-11 if your property or business is nearby.

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of 5035 Beresford Street in Burnaby's Royal Oak Area
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a rezoning application (REZ 25-11) for the property at 5035 Beresford Street in Burnaby. A rezoning changes the official land-use rules for a specific property, which could allow a different type of building or development there. The site sits within the Royal Oak Community Plan area, and the proposal is shown on planning sketches dated March 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on REZ 25-11 — it could affect nearby property values or development activity.

briefs.title: Proposed: 20-Storey Rental Apartment at 5035 Beresford Street Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a rezoning application that would allow a 20-storey rental apartment building with 215 units to be built at 5035 Beresford Street in the Royal Oak area. The site currently holds a printing shop, and the proposal would change its zoning — meaning the rules about what can be built there — from industrial to a residential development district.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council rezoning vote — this could affect nearby property owners and businesses on Beresford Street.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-11 rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading at Council

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on hiring an outside expert to review Burnaby's financial practices
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on bringing in an independent (outside, unbiased) reviewer to look closely at how the City plans its budget, manages day-to-day spending, and decides which big projects to fund. The review is meant to help protect the City's financial health and reduce the risk of steep tax hikes or rising service costs for businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — it could influence future tax increases affecting your business costs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — external financial review approval

briefs.title: Council to consider approving commemorative plaque for Burnaby Lake Overpass opening
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the official wording for a commemorative plaque marking the proposed opening of the Burnaby Lake Overpass on April 26, 2026. The plaque would recognize the pedestrian and cyclist bridge over Highway 1, linking Burnaby Lake and Deer Lake, and acknowledge funding partners including TransLink, the federal government, and the Province of BC.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Lake Overpass proposed official opening date

briefs.title: Council to vote on wording for Burnaby Lake Overpass commemorative plaque
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on the exact wording for a commemorative plaque to be installed at the new Burnaby Lake Overpass, a pedestrian and cyclist bridge over Highway 1. The overpass connects Burnaby Lake and Deer Lake trails, and its grand opening is scheduled for April 26, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14 if your business is near the overpass area.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Lake Overpass grand opening public event

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Renewing fire protection services contract for $1.67M
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with A.W. Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. to inspect, maintain, and repair fire protection and emergency lighting systems at City buildings. The proposed three-year renewal would run from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029, at an estimated cost of $1,666,061 including taxes.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if you supply fire protection services in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fire protection services contract renewal — proposed start date
Burnaby fire protection services contract renewal — proposed end date

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Cisco Software Renewal Worth $442,779 — 18.6% Price Increase
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing two Cisco software contracts — UCCX (a call-centre tool) and WebEx (a video and messaging platform) — used by City staff. The pending renewal would run through May 2029 and bring the total estimated contract value to roughly $816,000, reflecting an 18.6% annualized price increase over the previous term due to higher unit prices.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects City tech spending but has no direct compliance requirement for private businesses.

key_dates.label: Cisco WebEx contract renewal pending Council approval — starts
Cisco WebEx contract renewal — end of proposed term

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Renewing Cisco WebEx Contact Center software licence for $394,942
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on renewing a three-year software licence subscription with Long View Systems Corporation to keep Burnaby's phone-based customer service system running. The proposed contract would cost an estimated $394,942 (taxes included) and run from May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — no direct action required for most business owners.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Cisco WebEx Contact Center licence renewal — proposed start date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.04M contract increase for Burnaby building HVAC maintenance
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a contract increase of $1,043,700 (including tax) for Just Mechanical Ltd. to continue maintaining and repairing heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems at various City-owned buildings. The increase covers added work at new sites — including Fire Hall #8, the Christine Sinclair Community Centre, the Neighbourhood Resource Centre, and the Laurel Street Works Yard — bringing the total estimated contract value to $4,833,150.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if your business services City facilities or competes in mechanical contracting.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby HVAC Service Contract Renewal and Additional Funding
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing and expanding an HVAC (heating, ventilation, and air conditioning) maintenance contract for City facilities. The proposed renewal would extend the contract from April 1 to June 30, 2026, and add $579,600 in new spending — bringing the total estimated contract value to roughly $5.4 million since 2021.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the council decision if your business supplies HVAC services or bids on City maintenance contracts.

key_dates.label: Burnaby HVAC contract renewal #2 proposed start date
Burnaby HVAC contract renewal #2 proposed end date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract extension for Burnaby city building HVAC services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on extending its heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) maintenance and repair contract with Just Mechanical Ltd. for an estimated $579,600 including taxes. This is a temporary extension to keep city buildings running smoothly while the City runs a new competitive bidding process to find a long-term service provider.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — this affects HVAC service providers bidding on city contracts.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — HVAC contract extension approval

briefs.title: Council to vote on two-year Rogers cellular contract extension worth $1.1 million
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on extending its cellular phone and data services contract with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. for two more years (October 2023 to October 2025), at an estimated cost of $1,135,949 including taxes. The extension would cover staff mobile phones, iPads, vehicle data connections, and public Wi-Fi at Burnaby Public Library branches.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you supply telecom or mobile services to the City of Burnaby.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Telephony Contract Extension Worth Up to $450,000
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed month-to-month contract change that would extend the City's telephone services agreement for up to nine months at a cost of $450,000. This is the latest in a series of renewals for a contract that started in 2020 and has totalled nearly $3.5 million — no new vendor has been selected yet, so the City is continuing with its current provider while a longer-term solution is arranged.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this is a City internal contract and is unlikely to directly affect most small businesses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract extension with Rogers for city cellular services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on extending its cellular voice and data contract with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. by up to $450,000 (including taxes). This is a short-term, month-to-month bridge deal to keep city phones and data running while Burnaby finishes switching to a new service provider chosen through a public bidding process.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — this is a city internal contract and does not affect private businesses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M contract for Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving work
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. to replace 16 drainage culverts (underground pipes that carry water under the trail) and pave the Central Valley Greenway between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road. The work is already budgeted in the City's 2026–2030 Financial Plan, with funding split between City reserves and TransLink.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near the Central Valley Greenway trail.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Central Valley Greenway culvert contract approval

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M contract for Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. to replace an undersized culvert (a pipe or tunnel that carries water under a road or structure) at Jerry Rogers Creek. The new box culvert would be built to handle heavy rainfall expected once every 100 years, along with related water and sewer utility upgrades.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14, 2026 council meeting if your business is near Jerry Rogers Creek or Marine Drive — construction may affect access.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Jerry Rogers Creek culvert contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M contract for modular four-plex housing pilot in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-unit (four-plex) housing building at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The three-storey building would be factory-built off-site, then delivered and assembled on location — a method being tested to see if it can deliver affordable, low-carbon housing faster and more cost-effectively in low-density neighbourhoods.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — this project could signal future modular housing contracts in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award decision

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider statutory rights-of-way at Gaglardi Way and Pandora St
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on granting three proposed statutory rights-of-way (SRWs) — legal agreements that give the City specific access or use rights over portions of privately or City-owned land — at two sites: 1200 Gaglardi Way and 7950 Pandora Street, near Simon Fraser University on Burnaby Mountain. The sketches show the exact boundaries of the proposed SRW areas on each property.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 1200 Gaglardi Way or 7950 Pandora St, monitor the council decision.

briefs.title: Council to vote on granting FortisBC a right-of-way for natural gas works at Burnaby Mountain
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on allowing FortisBC Energy Inc. to access portions of two City-owned properties — 7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way — through a Statutory Right-of-Way (a legal permission letting a utility use part of someone else's land). This would let FortisBC install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure needed to service the Mintara restaurant and other facilities in the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed for most businesses — monitor the April 14, 2026 council decision for any site impacts.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — FortisBC Burnaby Mountain right-of-way approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Proposed Mandatory Energy Reporting for Large BC Commercial Buildings
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to review the Building Benchmark BC 2024 Annual Report, which calls on the BC provincial government to make energy benchmarking — tracking and publicly reporting how much energy a building uses — mandatory for large commercial and multi-family buildings starting in 2026. The report recommends phasing in the requirement, beginning with buildings over 200,000 sq ft and stepping down to buildings over 50,000 sq ft by year three.
briefs.action_required: If you own or manage a large commercial or multi-family building, prepare to track and report your building's energy use.

key_dates.label: Proposed BC mandatory building energy benchmarking program launch target

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby joining Building Benchmark BC Year 7 energy program
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on whether to continue the city's partnership with Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) for its seventh year. BBBC is a voluntary program that helps building owners track and compare their energy use and carbon emissions — at no extra cost to the city, using about $16,500 already set aside in the 2026 Planning and Development budget.
briefs.action_required: If you own a building in Burnaby, consider signing up for BBBC to get free energy and emissions data.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Building Benchmark BC Year 7 participation approval

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's First Housing Target Progress Report to BC Province
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving and submitting its first Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. The report covers September 2025 to February 2026 and shows 910 net new homes were completed in Burnaby during that period. Once approved by Council, the report must be sent to the Province and posted publicly on the City's website.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's April 14 vote — this report signals major housing growth and zoning changes coming to Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Target Progress Report — Council approval vote
Burnaby Development Permit system (Form & Character, Streamside, Tenant Protection) takes effect
Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw — expected Council consideration

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: BC Province Orders Burnaby to Build 10,240 New Homes by 2030
briefs.summary: The BC Minister of Housing has issued a Housing Target Order — a legally binding directive — requiring the City of Burnaby to complete at least 10,240 net new housing units between September 1, 2025 and August 31, 2030. Burnaby City Council is set to review this order, which sets yearly milestones and requires the city to report its progress regularly to the Province.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's response — zoning and development rules in Burnaby may change to meet these targets.

key_dates.label: BC Provincial Housing Target Order for Burnaby takes effect
Burnaby Housing Target Order — first progress report due (Period 1.1 ends)
Burnaby Housing Target Order — Year 1 progress report due (Period 1.2 ends)
Burnaby Housing Target Order — Year 2 progress report due
Burnaby Housing Target Order — Year 3 progress report due
Burnaby Housing Target Order — Year 4 progress report due
BC Provincial Housing Target Order for Burnaby ends

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on submitting Burnaby's 6-month housing target progress report to BC
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on authorizing staff to send Burnaby's first progress report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. The report covers September 2025 to February 2026 and shows roughly 910 new homes were completed — putting Burnaby on track so far, but rising construction costs, slower development starts, and a pause in provincial affordable housing funding are raising concerns about hitting future targets.
briefs.action_required: If you work in construction or housing development, watch this file — provincial funding cuts may delay projects you depend on.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Target Progress Report — Year 1b submission deadline to BC

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Bylaw Changes Would Set New Rules for E-Bikes and Electric Scooters
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on bylaw amendments that would create new rules for shared electric bikes and electric kick scooters (e-scooters) in the city. A proposed bylaw would define where these devices can be parked and ridden, ban their use on sidewalks and major roads unless a protected bike lane or multi-use pathway is available, and set a $200 fine for riding in areas not designated for such use.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if you operate near bike lanes or shared scooter zones, this could affect your business.

briefs.title: Council to vote on bylaw changes enabling e-bike share and e-scooter programs in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on amendments to three city bylaws that would officially allow a new E-Bike Share Pilot Program and set rules for the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program (already underway since January 2026). The proposed changes would define where e-bikes and e-scooters can be ridden and parked, create designated parking zones for shared e-bikes, and set fines for riding these devices in areas not approved for their use.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new riding and parking rules could affect your business if you operate near designated zones.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — e-bike share and e-scooter bylaw amendments vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Housing Authority Proposed 2026 Operating Budget
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review and vote on the Burnaby Housing Authority's proposed 2026 operating budget. This budget outlines how the housing authority plans to spend public funds on affordable housing operations in the coming year — decisions that could affect tenants, landlords, and property managers connected to city-run housing programs.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this could affect affordable housing funding and programs in Burnaby.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Housing Authority's First Audited Financial Statements
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review the first-ever audited financial statements for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA), covering its launch on June 14, 2024 through December 31, 2025. The BHA received over $37 million from the City and is actively developing two affordable and market rental housing projects — one on 10th Avenue and one on Marine Drive in Edmonds.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's decision on BHA funding — new housing supply in Burnaby could affect your rental market.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to Consider Burnaby Housing Authority's 2025 Financials and 2026 Budget
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) first-ever audited financial statements and its 2026 operating budget at the April 14, 2026 meeting. Accepting these documents would unlock $2 million in City funding for the BHA's second year of operations, supporting two new rental housing projects in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's decision — it could affect housing supply and rental availability in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: BHA $25M GIC investment matures — Burnaby Housing Authority

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby Housing Authority annual governance and board appointments for 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on routine annual housekeeping matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) — a city-owned housing company. If approved, Council would skip a formal annual general meeting (AGM), appoint KPMG as auditor, confirm December 12, 2026 as the official reference date, accept the BHA's 2025 financial statements, and formally appoint 10 directors to the BHA board for either two- or three-year terms.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — this affects how Burnaby's city-owned housing authority is governed in 2026.

key_dates.label: BHA annual reference date — Burnaby Housing Authority AGM waiver takes effect

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to Consider: North Road BIA Certificate of Sufficiency for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a Certificate of Sufficiency for the North Road Business Improvement Association (BIA) — a formal document confirming that enough eligible businesses in the area have supported the BIA's renewal or continuation for 2026. If approved, businesses along North Road within the BIA boundary would continue to pay a levy (an extra annual fee collected with property taxes) to fund local improvements and promotions.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is in the North Road BIA boundary — you may be subject to the annual BIA levy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — North Road BIA Certificate of Sufficiency vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: North Road BIA Renewal Blocked by Sufficient Owner Opposition
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report showing that enough property owners on North Road voted against renewing the Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where businesses pay an extra tax to fund local improvements. Because over 50% of property owners (by both number of properties and total property value) submitted formal opposition, the proposed renewal bylaw cannot move forward as planned.
briefs.action_required: If you own or rent in the North Road BIA, watch for updates — the BIA may attempt a new renewal process in fall 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA renewal bylaw — earliest possible new Council initiative

briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on appointing new Chief Election Officer for Burnaby's 2026 election
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the October 17, 2026 General Local Election. The previous officer is on leave and not expected to return in time, so Council will consider replacing her with Back, an experienced administrator who has worked for the City of Burnaby before.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects who will run Burnaby's 2026 municipal election.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 General Local Election day

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Declaring April 28 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured'
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a request from the New Westminster & District Labour Council to officially declare April 28 as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job.' The request also asks Council to observe a minute of silence at their April meeting, encourage staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28, and allow a union member to make a short presentation on workplace health and safety issues at the April 14, 2026 Council meeting.
briefs.action_required: If workplace safety affects your business, consider attending the April 14 Council meeting to hear the presentation.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Day of Mourning delegation presentation
Day of Mourning for Workers — April 28 observance date

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Declaring April 28th Day of Mourning for Injured and Killed Workers
briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from the New Westminster & District Labour Council to officially declare April 28th as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job.' The group is also asking Council to observe a minute of silence at the April 14, 2026 evening meeting, and to encourage city staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28th.
briefs.action_required: If worker safety matters to your business, attend the April 14 Council meeting to hear the presentation.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council delegation presentation — Day of Mourning request
Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job — April 28

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Construction & Development, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to urge federal action on chlorine rail transport safety through Burnaby
briefs.summary: At a Special Council meeting on April 1, 2026, Burnaby Council unanimously voted to send a letter to the federal Minister of Transport raising serious safety concerns about liquid chlorine being transported by rail through densely populated areas of the Lower Mainland. The concern centres on a North Vancouver chlorine facility whose bylaw amendment — a change to local rules that would let it keep producing liquid chlorine past 2030 — is still awaiting a final vote, scheduled for April 13, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch the District of North Vancouver's April 13 vote — it could affect chlorine rail traffic through your area.

key_dates.label: District of North Vancouver — Chemtrade chlorine bylaw amendment third reading

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Approves Rezoning, Housing, and Infrastructure Contracts at March 24 Meeting
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on a range of items including rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage developments, a $4.2M water metering contract, hired equipment services, and endorsement of a Poverty Reduction Strategy. Several rezoning bylaws were advanced to first and second reading — meaning they are moving forward in the approval process but are not yet final law.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 6535 Kingsway, 2757 Norland Ave, or 5058 Imperial St, watch for upcoming public processes on those rezonings.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics
